34: // MaxLockWaitQueueLength sets the maximum length of a lock wait-queue that a
35: // read-write request is willing to enter and wait in. Used to provide a release
36: // valve and ensure some level of quality-of-service under severe per-key
37: // contention. If set to a non-zero value and an existing lock wait-queue is
38: // already equal to or exceeding this length, the request will be rejected
39: // eagerly instead of entering the queue and waiting.
40: //
41: // This is a fairly blunt mechanism to place an upper bound on resource
42: // utilization per lock wait-queue and ensure some reasonable level of
43: // quality-of-service for transactions that enter a lock wait-queue. More
44: // sophisticated queueing alternatives exist that account for queueing time and
45: // detect sustained queue growth before rejecting:
46: // - https://queue.acm.org/detail.cfm?id=2209336
47: // - https://queue.acm.org/detail.cfm?id=2839461
48: //
49: // We could explore these algorithms if this setting is too coarse grained and
50: // not serving its purpose well enough.
51: //
52: // Alternatively, we could implement the lock_timeout session variable that
53: // exists in Postgres (#67513) and use that to ensure quality-of-service for
54: // requests that wait for locks. With that configuration, this cluster setting
55: // would be relegated to a guardrail that protects against unbounded resource
56: // utilization and runaway queuing for misbehaving clients, a role it is well
57: // positioned to serve.
58: var MaxLockWaitQueueLength = settings.RegisterIntSetting(
59: 	settings.SystemOnly,
60: 	"kv.lock_table.maximum_lock_wait_queue_length",
61: 	"the maximum length of a lock wait-queue that read-write requests are willing "+
62: 		"to enter and wait in. The setting can be used to ensure some level of quality-of-service "+
63: 		"under severe per-key contention. If set to a non-zero value and an existing lock "+
64: 		"wait-queue is already equal to or exceeding this length, requests will be rejected "+
65: 		"eagerly instead of entering the queue and waiting. Set to 0 to disable.",
66: 	0,
67: 	settings.WithValidateInt(func(v int64) error {
68: 		if v < 0 {
69: 			return errors.Errorf("cannot be set to a negative value: %d", v)
70: 		}
71: 		if v == 0 {
72: 			return nil // disabled
73: 		}
74: 		// Don't let the setting be dropped below a reasonable value that we don't
75: 		// expect to impact internal transaction processing.
76: 		const minSafeMaxLength = 3
77: 		if v < minSafeMaxLength {
78: 			return errors.Errorf("cannot be set below %d: %d", minSafeMaxLength, v)
79: 		}
80: 		return nil
81: 	}),
82: )
83: 
84: // DiscoveredLocksThresholdToConsultTxnStatusCache sets a threshold as mentioned
85: // in the description string. The default of 200 is somewhat arbitrary but
86: // should suffice for small OLTP transactions. Given the default
87: // 10,000 lock capacity of the lock table, 200 is small enough to not matter
88: // much against the capacity, which is desirable. We have seen examples with
89: // discoveredCount > 100,000, caused by stats collection, where we definitely
90: // want to avoid adding these locks to the lock table, if possible.
91: var DiscoveredLocksThresholdToConsultTxnStatusCache = settings.RegisterIntSetting(
92: 	settings.SystemOnly,
93: 	// NOTE: the name of this setting mentions "finalized" for historical reasons.
94: 	"kv.lock_table.discovered_locks_threshold_for_consulting_finalized_txn_cache",
95: 	"the maximum number of discovered locks by a waiter, above which the txn status cache"+
96: 		"is consulted and resolvable locks are not added to the lock table -- this should be a small"+
97: 		"fraction of the maximum number of locks in the lock table",
98: 	200,
99: 	settings.NonNegativeInt,
100: )

55: // TestConcurrencyManagerBasic verifies that sequences of requests interacting
56: // with a concurrency manager perform properly.
57: //
58: // The input files use the following DSL:
59: //
60: // new-txn      name=<txn-name> ts=<int>[,<int>] [epoch=<int>] [iso=<level>] [priority=<priority>] [uncertainty-limit=<int>[,<int>]]
61: // new-request  name=<req-name> txn=<txn-name>|none ts=<int>[,<int>] [priority=<priority>] [inconsistent] [wait-policy=<policy>] [lock-timeout] [deadlock-timeout] [max-lock-wait-queue-length=<int>] [poison-policy=[err|wait]]
62: //
63: //	<proto-name> [<field-name>=<field-value>...] (hint: see scanSingleRequest)
64: //
65: // sequence     req=<req-name> [eval-kind=<pess|opt|pess-after-opt]
66: // poison       req=<req-name>
67: // finish       req=<req-name>
68: //
69: // handle-lock-conflict-error  req=<req-name> txn=<txn-name> key=<key> lease-seq=<seq>
70: // handle-txn-push-error       req=<req-name> txn=<txn-name> key=<key>  TODO(nvanbenschoten): implement this
71: //
72: // check-opt-no-conflicts            req=<req-name>
73: // is-key-locked-by-conflicting-txn  req=<req-name> key=<key> strength=<strength>
74: //
75: // on-lock-acquired  req=<req-name> key=<key> [seq=<seq>] [dur=r|u] [str=<strength>]
76: // on-lock-updated   req=<req-name> txn=<txn-name> key=<key> status=[committed|aborted|pending] [ts=<int>[,<int>]] [ignored-seqs=<int>[-<int>][,<int>[-<int>]]
77: // on-txn-updated    txn=<txn-name> status=[committed|aborted|pending] [ts=<int>[,<int>]]
78: //
79: // on-lease-updated  leaseholder=<bool> lease-seq=<seq>
80: // on-split
81: // on-merge
82: // on-snapshot-applied
83: //
84: // debug-latch-manager
85: // debug-lock-table
86: // debug-disable-txn-pushes
87: // debug-set-clock           ts=<secs>
88: // debug-advance-clock       ts=<secs>
89: // debug-set-discovered-locks-threshold-to-consult-txn-status-cache n=<count>
90: // debug-set-batch-pushed-lock-resolution-enabled ok=<enabled>
91: // debug-set-max-locks n=<count>
92: // reset [namespace|force]
93: func TestConcurrencyManagerBasic(t *testing.T) {
94: 	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
95: 	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)
96: 
97: 	datadriven.Walk(t, datapathutils.TestDataPath(t, "concurrency_manager"), func(t *testing.T, path string) {
98: 		c := newCluster()
99: 		c.enableTxnPushes()
100: 		m := concurrency.NewManager(c.makeConfig())
101: 		m.OnRangeLeaseUpdated(1, true /* isLeaseholder */) // enable
102: 		c.m = m
103: 		mon := newMonitor()
104: 		datadriven.RunTest(t, path, func(t *testing.T, d *datadriven.TestData) string {
105: 			switch d.Cmd {
106: 			case "new-txn":
107: 				var txnName string
108: 				d.ScanArgs(t, "name", &txnName)
109: 				ts := scanTimestamp(t, d)
110: 
111: 				epoch := 0
112: 				if d.HasArg("epoch") {
113: 					d.ScanArgs(t, "epoch", &epoch)
114: 				}
115: 
116: 				iso := concurrency.ScanIsoLevel(t, d)
117: 				priority := scanTxnPriority(t, d)
118: 
119: 				uncertaintyLimit := ts
120: 				if d.HasArg("uncertainty-limit") {
121: 					uncertaintyLimit = scanTimestampWithName(t, d, "uncertainty-limit")
122: 				}
123: 
124: 				txn, ok := c.txnsByName[txnName]
125: 				var id uuid.UUID
126: 				if ok {
127: 					id = txn.ID
128: 				} else {
129: 					id = c.newTxnID()
130: 				}
131: 				txn = &roachpb.Transaction{
132: 					TxnMeta: enginepb.TxnMeta{
133: 						ID:             id,
134: 						IsoLevel:       iso,
135: 						Epoch:          enginepb.TxnEpoch(epoch),
136: 						WriteTimestamp: ts,
137: 						MinTimestamp:   ts,
138: 						Priority:       priority,
139: 					},
140: 					ReadTimestamp:          ts,
141: 					GlobalUncertaintyLimit: uncertaintyLimit,
142: 				}
143: 				c.registerTxn(txnName, txn)
144: 				return ""
145: 
146: 			case "new-request":
147: 				var reqName string
148: 				d.ScanArgs(t, "name", &reqName)
149: 				if _, ok := c.requestsByName[reqName]; ok {
150: 					d.Fatalf(t, "duplicate request: %s", reqName)
151: 				}
152: 
153: 				var txnName string
154: 				d.ScanArgs(t, "txn", &txnName)
155: 				txn, ok := c.txnsByName[txnName]
156: 				if !ok && txnName != "none" {
157: 					d.Fatalf(t, "unknown txn %s", txnName)
158: 				}
159: 
160: 				ts := scanTimestamp(t, d)
161: 				if txn != nil {
162: 					txn = txn.Clone()
163: 					txn.ReadTimestamp = ts
164: 					txn.WriteTimestamp = ts
165: 				}
166: 
167: 				readConsistency := kvpb.CONSISTENT
168: 				if d.HasArg("inconsistent") {
169: 					readConsistency = kvpb.INCONSISTENT
170: 				}
171: 
172: 				priority := scanUserPriority(t, d)
173: 				waitPolicy := scanWaitPolicy(t, d, false /* required */)
174: 
175: 				var lockTimeout time.Duration
176: 				if d.HasArg("lock-timeout") {
177: 					// A lock timeout of 1ns will be considered immediately expired
178: 					// without a delay by the lockTableWaiter, ensuring that the lock
179: 					// timeout logic deterministically fires.
180: 					// See (*lockTableWaiterImpl).timeUntilDeadline.
181: 					lockTimeout = 1 * time.Nanosecond
182: 				}
183: 
184: 				var maxLockWaitQueueLength int
185: 				if d.HasArg("max-lock-wait-queue-length") {
186: 					d.ScanArgs(t, "max-lock-wait-queue-length", &maxLockWaitQueueLength)
187: 				}
188: 
189: 				var deadlockTimeout time.Duration
190: 				if d.HasArg("deadlock-timeout") {
191: 					d.ScanArgs(t, "deadlock-timeout", &deadlockTimeout)
192: 				}
193: 
194: 				ba := &kvpb.BatchRequest{}
195: 				pp := scanPoisonPolicy(t, d)
196: 
197: 				// Each kvpb.Request is provided on an indented line.
198: 				reqs, reqUnions := scanRequests(t, d, c)
199: 				ba.Txn = txn
200: 				ba.Timestamp = ts

32: // Default upper bound on the number of locks in a lockTable.
33: const defaultLockTableSize = 10000
34: 
35: // The kind of waiting that the request is subject to.
36: type waitKind int
37: 
38: const (
39: 	_ waitKind = iota
40: 
41: 	// waitFor indicates that the request is waiting on another transaction to
42: 	// release its locks or complete its own request. waitingStates with this
43: 	// waitKind will provide information on who the request is waiting on. The
44: 	// request will likely want to eventually push the conflicting transaction.
45: 	waitFor
46: 
47: 	// waitElsewhere is used when the lockTable is under memory pressure and is
48: 	// clearing its internal queue state. Like the waitFor* states, it informs
49: 	// the request who it is waiting for so that deadlock detection works.
50: 	// However, sequencing information inside the lockTable is mostly discarded.
51: 	waitElsewhere
52: 
53: 	// waitSelf indicates that a different request from the same transaction has
54: 	// claimed (but not acquired) the lock already. This request should sit tight
55: 	// and wait for a new notification without pushing anyone.
56: 	//
57: 	// It must be the case that there are no lock holders for the associated key
58: 	// if a request is actively waiting in the waitSelf state. That's because if
59: 	// the key is locked by another transaction, the request must be in the
60: 	// waitFor state. A request does not need to actively wait if the only lock
61: 	// that's held on the key belongs to its own transaction, thus making the
62: 	// waitSelf state impossible for held locks.
63: 	//
64: 	// NB: The main motivation of the waitSelf state is to avoid certain
65: 	// dependency cycles by potentially re-ordering the lock's wait queue.
66: 	// Typically, the waitSelf state arises when there are >= 1 locking requests
67: 	// from other transactions between two requests from the same transaction
68: 	// waiting in a lock's wait queue. So something like:
69: 	//
70: 	// key a: unlocked
71: 	// wait-queue: [(r1,txn1,inactive), (r2,txn2,active), ... (r3,txn1,active)]
72: 	//
73: 	// There is a dependency cycle between the requests from txn1 and txn2 here.
74: 	// Instead of forcing one of the two transaction's to abort, we're able to
75: 	// break the dependency cycle by having r3 wait and sit tight in the waitSelf
76: 	// state[1]. Now, if r1 acquires the lock, r3 will be re-ordered[2] to proceed
77: 	// before r2 (thus breaking the dependency cycle); if it doesn't, r2 will no
78: 	// longer depend on txn1 and can thus proceed (i.e. no more dependency cycle).
79: 	//
80: 	// [1] A transaction can't push itself, but if it finds the request at the
81: 	// head of the queue everyone is pushing belongs to its own transaction, it
82: 	// knows there is a local dependency cycle.
83: 	//
84: 	// [2] Note that re-ordering happens only when r1 acquires the lock, so the
85: 	// waitSelf state is a mechanism by which r3 is able to sit tight without
86: 	// pushing anyone.
87: 	waitSelf
88: 
89: 	// waitQueueMaxLengthExceeded indicates that the request attempted to enter a
90: 	// lock wait-queue as a locking request and found that the queue's length was
91: 	// already equal to or exceeding the request's configured maximum. As a
92: 	// result, the request was rejected.
93: 	waitQueueMaxLengthExceeded
94: 
95: 	// doneWaiting indicates that the request is done waiting on this pass
96: 	// through the lockTable and should make another call to ScanAndEnqueue.
97: 	doneWaiting
98: )
99: 
100: // The current waiting state of the request.
101: //
102: // See the detailed comment about "Waiting logic" on lockTableGuardImpl.
103: type waitingState struct {
104: 	kind waitKind
105: 
106: 	// Fields below are only populated for waitFor*, waitElsewhere and
107: 	// waitQueueMaxLengthExceeded kinds.
108: 
109: 	// Represents who the request is waiting for. The conflicting
110: 	// transaction may be a lock holder of a conflicting lock or a
111: 	// conflicting request being sequenced through the same lockTable.
112: 	txn                   *enginepb.TxnMeta // always non-nil
113: 	key                   roachpb.Key       // the key of the conflict
114: 	held                  bool              // is the conflict a held lock?
115: 	queuedLockingRequests int               // how many locking requests are waiting?
116: 	queuedReaders         int               // how many readers are waiting?
117: 
118: 	// Represents the lock strength of the action that the request was trying to
119: 	// perform when it hit the conflict. E.g. was it trying to perform a (possibly
120: 	// locking) read or write an Intent?
121: 	guardStrength lock.Strength
122: }
123: 
124: // String implements the fmt.Stringer interface.
125: func (s waitingState) String() string {
126: 	return redact.StringWithoutMarkers(s)
127: }
128: 
129: // SafeFormat implements the redact.SafeFormatter interface.
130: func (s waitingState) SafeFormat(w redact.SafePrinter, _ rune) {
131: 	switch s.kind {
132: 	case waitFor:
133: 		target := redact.SafeString("holding lock")
134: 		if !s.held {
135: 			target = "running request"
136: 		}
137: 		w.Printf("wait for txn %s %s @ key %s (queuedLockingRequests: %d, queuedReaders: %d)",
138: 			s.txn.Short(), target, s.key, s.queuedLockingRequests, s.queuedReaders)
139: 	case waitSelf:
140: 		w.Printf("wait self @ key %s", s.key)
141: 	case waitElsewhere:
142: 		if !s.held {
143: 			w.SafeString("wait elsewhere by proceeding to evaluation")
144: 		}
145: 		w.Printf("wait elsewhere for txn %s @ key %s", s.txn.Short(), s.key)
146: 	case waitQueueMaxLengthExceeded:
147: 		w.Printf("wait-queue maximum length exceeded @ key %s with length %d",
148: 			s.key, s.queuedLockingRequests)
149: 	case doneWaiting:
150: 		w.SafeString("done waiting")
151: 	default:
152: 		panic("unhandled waitingState.kind")
153: 	}
154: }
155: 
156: // Implementation
157: // TODO(sbhola):
158: // - metrics about lockTable state to export to observability debug pages:
159: //   number of locks, number of waiting requests, wait time?, ...
160: 
161: // The btree for keys that have locks on them.
162: type treeMu struct {
163: 	mu syncutil.RWMutex // Protects everything in this struct.
164: 
165: 	// For assigning sequence numbers to the keyLocks objects as required by
166: 	// the util/interval/generic type contract.
167: 	lockIDSeqNum uint64
168: 
169: 	// Container for all keyLocks structs, inside which per-key lock tracking
170: 	// is done. This includes information about the lock holder(s), requests being
171: 	// sequenced through the lock table, and any queues that form on the key
172: 	// because of read-write or write-write conflicts. Empty[1] keyLocks structs
173: 	// are garbage collected. Additionally, keys which only have replicated locks
174: 	// on them and no contending requests may also be garbage collected, as their
175: 	// state can be recovered from persistent storage.
176: 	//
177: 	// [1] Keys that are not locked and have an empty wait-queue.
178: 	btree
179: 
180: 	// numKeysLocked tracks the number of keyLocks structs in the b-tree. It is
181: 	// primarily used for constraining memory consumption. Ideally, we should be
182: 	// doing better memory accounting than this.
183: 	numKeysLocked atomic.Int64
184: 
185: 	// For dampening the frequency with which we enforce
186: 	// lockTableImpl.maxKeysLocked.
187: 	lockAddMaxLocksCheckInterval uint64
188: }
189: 
190: // lockTableImpl is an implementation of lockTable.
191: //
192: // Concurrency: in addition to holding latches, we require for a particular
193: // request ScanAndEnqueue() and CurState() must be called by the same
194: // thread.
195: //
196: // Mutex ordering:   lockTableImpl.enabledMu
197: //
198: //	> treeMu.mu
199: //	> keyLocks.mu
200: //	> lockTableGuardImpl.mu
201: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/cockroach--v24.3.0@2e3aaf185d0ed9361efdbf04cd7cd1153477d912/-/blob/pkg/kv/kvserver/concurrency/lock_table.go%3FL1-200"}

48: /*
49: Test needs to handle caller constraints wrt latches being held. The datadriven
50: test uses the following format:
51: 
52: new-lock-table maxlocks=<int>
53: ----
54: 
55:   Creates a lockTable. The lockTable is initially enabled.
56: 
57: time-tick [m=<int>] [s=<int>] [ms=<int>] [ns=<int>]
58: ----
59: 
60:   Forces the manual clock to tick forward m minutes, s seconds, ms milliseconds, and ns nanoseconds.
61: 
62: new-txn txn=<name> ts=<int>[,<int>] epoch=<int> [seq=<int>] [iso=<level>]
63: ----
64: 
65:  Creates a TxnMeta.
66: 
67: new-request r=<name> txn=<name>|none ts=<int>[,<int>] spans=none|shared|update|exclusive|intent@<start>[,<end>]+... [skip-locked] [max-lock-wait-queue-length=<int>]
68: ----
69: 
70:  Creates a Request.
71: 
72: scan r=<name>
73: ----
74: start-waiting: <bool>
75: 
76:  Calls lockTable.ScanAndEnqueue. If the request has an existing guard, uses it.
77:  If a guard is returned, stores it for later use.
78: 
79: scan-opt r=<name>
80: ----
81: start-waiting: <bool>
82: 
83:  Calls lockTable.ScanOptimistic. The request must not have an existing guard.
84:  If a guard is returned, stores it for later use.
85: 
86: acquire r=<name> k=<key> durability=r|u [ignored-seqs=<int>[-<int>][,<int>[-<int>]] strength=<strength>
87: ----
88: <error string>
89: 
90:  Acquires lock for the request, using the existing guard for that request.
91: 
92: release txn=<name> span=<start>[,<end>]
93: ----
94: <error string>
95: 
96:  Releases locks for the named transaction.
97: 
98: update txn=<name> ts=<int>[,<int>] epoch=<int> span=<start>[,<end>] [ignored-seqs=<int>[-<int>][,<int>[-<int>]]]
99: ----
100: <error string>
101: 
102:  Updates locks for the named transaction.
103: 
104: pushed-txn-updated txn=<name> status=committed|aborted|pending [ts=<ts>]
105: ----
106: 
107:  Informs the lock table that the named transaction is finalized.
108: 
109: add-discovered r=<name> k=<key> txn=<name> [lease-seq=<seq>] [consult-txn-status-cache=<bool>] [strength=<strength>]
110: ----
111: <error string>
112: 
113:  Adds a discovered lock that is discovered by the named request.
114: 
115: check-opt-no-conflicts r=<name> spans=none|shared|update|exclusive|intent@<start>[,<end>]+...
116: ----
117: no-conflicts: <bool>
118: 
119:  Checks whether the request, which previously called ScanOptimistic, has no lock conflicts.
120: 
121: is-key-locked-by-conflicting-txn r=<name> k=<key> strength=<strength>
122: ----
123: locked: <bool>
124: 
125:  Checks whether the provided key is locked by a conflicting transaction.
126: 
127: dequeue r=<name>
128: ----
129: <error string>
130: 
131:  Calls lockTable.Dequeue for the named request. The request and guard are
132:  discarded after this.
133: 
134: guard-state r=<name>
135: ----
136: new|old: state=<state> [txn=<name> ts=<ts>]
137: 
138:   Calls lockTableGuard.NewStateChan in a non-blocking manner, followed by
139:   CurState.
140: 
141: should-wait r=<name>
142: ----
143: <bool>
144: 
145:  Calls lockTableGuard.ShouldWait.
146: 
147: resolve-before-scanning r=<name>
148: ----
149: <intents to resolve>
150: 
151: enable [lease-seq=<seq>]
152: ----
153: 
154:  Calls lockTable.Enable.
155: 
156: clear [disable]
157: ----
158: <state of lock table>
159: 
160:  Calls lockTable.Clear. Optionally disables the lockTable.
161: 
162: print
163: ----
164: <state of lock table>
165: 
166: query span=<start>[,<end> | /Max] [max-locks=<int>] [max-bytes=<int>] [uncontended]
167: ----
168: 
169:  Queries the lockTable over a given span (or over the entire LT if no span
170:  provided), returning lock state info up to a maximum number of locks or bytes
171:  if provided.  By default only returns contended locks (those with waiters),
172:  unless the uncontended option is given.
173: 
174: 
175: metrics
176: ----
177: <metrics for lock table>
178: 
179:  Calls lockTable.String.
180: */
181: 
182: func TestLockTableBasic(t *testing.T) {
183: 	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
184: 	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)
185: 
186: 	datadriven.Walk(t, datapathutils.TestDataPath(t, "lock_table"), func(t *testing.T, path string) {
187: 		var lt lockTable
188: 		var txnsByName map[string]*enginepb.TxnMeta
189: 		var txnCounter uint128.Uint128
190: 		var requestsByName map[string]Request
191: 		var guardsByReqName map[string]lockTableGuard
192: 		manualClock := timeutil.NewManualTime(timeutil.Unix(0, 123))
193: 		clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(manualClock)
194: 		datadriven.RunTest(t, path, func(t *testing.T, d *datadriven.TestData) string {
195: 			switch d.Cmd {
196: 			case "new-lock-table":
197: 				var maxLocks int
198: 				d.ScanArgs(t, "maxlocks", &maxLocks)
199: 				ltImpl := newLockTable(
200: 					int64(maxLocks), roachpb.RangeID(3), clock, cluster.MakeTestingClusterSettings(),
201: 				)
202: 				ltImpl.enabled = true
203: 				ltImpl.enabledSeq = 1
204: 				ltImpl.minKeysLocked = 0
205: 				lt = maybeWrapInVerifyingLockTable(ltImpl)
206: 				txnsByName = make(map[string]*enginepb.TxnMeta)
207: 				txnCounter = uint128.FromInts(0, 0)
208: 				requestsByName = make(map[string]Request)
209: 				guardsByReqName = make(map[string]lockTableGuard)
210: 				return ""
211: 
212: 			case "time-tick":
213: 				var timeDelta time.Duration
214: 				var delta int
215: 				if d.HasArg("m") {
216: 					d.ScanArgs(t, "m", &delta)
217: 					timeDelta += time.Duration(delta) * time.Minute
218: 				}
219: 				if d.HasArg("s") {
220: 					d.ScanArgs(t, "s", &delta)
221: 					timeDelta += time.Duration(delta) * time.Second
222: 				}
223: 				if d.HasArg("ms") {
224: 					d.ScanArgs(t, "ms", &delta)
225: 					timeDelta += time.Duration(delta) * time.Millisecond
226: 				}
227: 				if d.HasArg("ns") {
228: 					d.ScanArgs(t, "ns", &delta)
229: 					timeDelta += time.Duration(delta)
230: 				}
231: 				manualClock.Advance(timeDelta)
232: 				return ""
233: 
234: 			case "new-txn":
235: 				// UUIDs for transactions are numbered from 1 by this test code and
236: 				// lockTableImpl.String() knows about UUIDs and not transaction names.
237: 				// Assigning txnNames of the form txn1, txn2, ... keeps the two in sync,
238: 				// which makes test cases easier to understand.
239: 				var txnName string
240: 				d.ScanArgs(t, "txn", &txnName)
241: 				ts := scanTimestamp(t, d)
242: 				var epoch int
243: 				d.ScanArgs(t, "epoch", &epoch)
244: 				var seq int
245: 				if d.HasArg("seq") {
246: 					d.ScanArgs(t, "seq", &seq)
247: 				}
248: 				iso := ScanIsoLevel(t, d)
249: 				txnMeta, ok := txnsByName[txnName]
250: 				var id uuid.UUID

35: // LockTableDeadlockOrLivenessDetectionPushDelay sets the delay before pushing
36: // in order to detect dependency cycles between transactions or coordinator
37: // failure of conflicting transactions.
38: var LockTableDeadlockOrLivenessDetectionPushDelay = settings.RegisterDurationSetting(
39: 	settings.SystemOnly,
40: 	"kv.lock_table.deadlock_detection_push_delay",
41: 	"the delay before pushing in order to detect dependency cycles between transactions",
42: 	// Transactions that come across contending locks must push the lock holder
43: 	// to detect whether they're abandoned or are part of a dependency cycle that
44: 	// would cause a deadlock. We optimistically assume failed transaction
45: 	// coordinators and deadlocks are not common in production applications, and
46: 	// wait locally on locks for a while before checking for a deadlock. This
47: 	// reduces needless pushing in cases where there is no deadlock or coordinator
48: 	// failure to speak of.
49: 	//
50: 	// Assuming we care about detecting failed coordinators more than deadlocks
51: 	// (which are a true rarity and a case we don't want to optimize for),
52: 	// increasing the value of this setting would come down to optimizing the cost
53: 	// we pay per-abandoned transaction. Currently, with the txnStatusCache,
54: 	// we pay a cost per abandoned transaction per range. The txnStatusCache is
55: 	// also quite small. Some improvements here could be:
56: 	// - A per-store cache instead of a per-range cache.
57: 	// - Increasing the number of finalized transactions we keep track of, even if
58: 	// this is per-request instead of per-range.
59: 	//
60: 	// The deadlock portion of this setting is analogous to Postgres'
61: 	// deadlock_timeout setting, which has a default value of 1s:
62: 	//  https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/runtime-config-locks.html#GUC-DEADLOCK-TIMEOUT.
63: 	//
64: 	// When increasing this value, we should be conscious that even once
65: 	// distributed deadlock detection begins, there is some latency proportional
66: 	// to the length of the dependency cycle before the deadlock is detected.
67: 	//
68: 	//
69: 	// TODO(nvanbenschoten): increasing this default value.
70: 	100*time.Millisecond,
71: 	settings.WithName("kv.lock_table.deadlock_detection_or_liveness_push_delay"),
72: 	// Old setting name when liveness and deadlock push delays could be set
73: 	// independently.
74: 	settings.WithRetiredName("kv.lock_table.coordinator_liveness_push_delay"),
75: )

77: // lockTableWaiterImpl is an implementation of lockTableWaiter.
78: type lockTableWaiterImpl struct {
79: 	nodeDesc *roachpb.NodeDescriptor
80: 	st       *cluster.Settings
81: 	clock    *hlc.Clock
82: 	stopper  *stop.Stopper
83: 	ir       IntentResolver
84: 	lt       lockTable
85: 
86: 	// When set, WriteIntentErrors are propagated instead of pushing
87: 	// conflicting transactions.
88: 	disableTxnPushing bool
89: 	// When set, called just before each push timer event is processed.
90: 	onPushTimer func()
91: }
92: 
93: // IntentResolver is an interface used by lockTableWaiterImpl to push
94: // transactions and to resolve intents. It contains only the subset of the
95: // intentresolver.IntentResolver interface that lockTableWaiterImpl needs.
96: type IntentResolver interface {
97: 	// PushTransaction pushes the provided transaction. The method will push the
98: 	// provided pushee transaction immediately, if possible. Otherwise, it will
99: 	// block until the pushee transaction is finalized or eventually can be
100: 	// pushed successfully.
101: 	PushTransaction(
102: 		context.Context, *enginepb.TxnMeta, kvpb.Header, kvpb.PushTxnType,
103: 	) (*roachpb.Transaction, bool, *Error)
104: 
105: 	// ResolveIntent synchronously resolves the provided intent.
106: 	ResolveIntent(context.Context, roachpb.LockUpdate, intentresolver.ResolveOptions) *Error
107: 
108: 	// ResolveIntents synchronously resolves the provided batch of intents.
109: 	ResolveIntents(context.Context, []roachpb.LockUpdate, intentresolver.ResolveOptions) *Error
110: }

112: // WaitOn implements the lockTableWaiter interface.
113: func (w *lockTableWaiterImpl) WaitOn(
114: 	ctx context.Context, req Request, guard lockTableGuard,
115: ) *Error {
116: 	newStateC := guard.NewStateChan()
117: 	ctxDoneC := ctx.Done()
118: 	shouldQuiesceC := w.stopper.ShouldQuiesce()
119: 	// Used to delay liveness and deadlock detection pushes.
120: 	var timer timeutil.Timer
121: 	defer timer.Stop()
122: 	var timerC <-chan time.Time
123: 	var timerWaitingState waitingState
124: 	// Used to enforce lock timeouts.
125: 	var lockDeadline time.Time
126: 
127: 	tracer := newContentionEventTracer(tracing.SpanFromContext(ctx), w.clock)
128: 	// Make sure the contention time info is finalized when exiting the function.
129: 	defer tracer.notify(ctx, waitingState{kind: doneWaiting})

131: 	for {
132: 		select {
133: 		// newStateC will be signaled for the transaction we are currently
134: 		// contending on. We will continue to receive updates about this
135: 		// transaction until it no longer contends with us, at which point
136: 		// either one of the other channels fires or we receive state
137: 		// about another contending transaction on newStateC.
138: 		case <-newStateC:
139: 			timerC = nil
140: 			state, err := guard.CurState()
141: 			if err != nil {
142: 				return kvpb.NewError(err)
143: 			}
144: 			log.VEventf(ctx, 3, "lock wait-queue event: %s", state)
145: 			tracer.notify(ctx, state)
146: 			switch state.kind {
147: 			case waitFor:
148: 				// waitFor indicates that the request is waiting on another
149: 				// transaction. This transaction may be the lock holder of a
150: 				// conflicting lock or the head of a lock-wait queue that the

// Copyright 2020 The Cockroach Authors.
//
// Use of this software is governed by the CockroachDB Software License
// included in the /LICENSE file.

package concurrency

import (
	"context"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvpb"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/concurrency/isolation"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/concurrency/lock"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/lockspanset"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/spanset"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/txnwait"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/roachpb"
	clustersettings "github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/settings/cluster"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/storage/enginepb"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/hlc"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/leaktest"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/log"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/uuid"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// TestWriteWriteConflictDetection verifies that the lock table correctly detects
// write-write conflicts between two transactions.
func TestWriteWriteConflictDetection(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	// Create a lock table with capacity for this test
	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	// Create two transactions with different UUIDs
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// Create requests for the same key with intent locks
	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// First request: txn1 writes to key1
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Second request: txn2 writes to the same key1
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Scan and enqueue first request
	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, guard1)
	require.False(t, guard1.ShouldWait(), "first request should not wait")

	// Acquire lock for first request
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Scan and enqueue second request - should find conflict
	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, guard2)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "second request should wait for first transaction's lock")

	// Check the waiting state
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state.kind)
	require.Equal(t, txn1.ID, state.txn.ID)
	require.True(t, state.held)

	// Release the locks
	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestReadWriteConflictWithSerializableIsolation verifies that serializable
// transactions detect read-write conflicts.
func TestReadWriteConflictWithSerializableIsolation(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction writes to key
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.False(t, guard1.ShouldWait())

	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to read the same key
	// In serializable isolation, reads should also detect conflicts with writes
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Shared, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, guard2)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "read should wait for write lock")

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestTransactionPriorityConflictResolution verifies that transaction priority
// influences conflict resolution.
func TestTransactionPriorityConflictResolution(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Low priority transaction writes first
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.LowUserPriority,
	}

	// High priority transaction tries to write to the same key
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.HighUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction (low priority) acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction (high priority) tries to write
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// High priority transaction should wait, but could potentially push low priority
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, txn1.ID, state.txn.ID)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestLockAcquisitionTimout verifies that lock acquisition can timeout.
func TestLockAcquisitionTimeout(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to acquire with a timeout
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:            txn2,
		Timestamp:      txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:      lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans:     spanset.SpanSet{},
		LockWaitPolicy: lock.WaitPolicy_Block,
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	// In a real scenario with a deadline, the wait would eventually timeout
	// For this test, we just verify the wait state is set up correctly
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state.kind)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestIntentResolutionAfterCommit verifies that locks are released after
// transaction commit.
func TestIntentResolutionAfterCommit(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to access the same key
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "should wait for first transaction's lock")

	// Release the lock from first transaction (simulating commit)
	lt.Dequeue(guard1)

	// After dequeue, second transaction's wait state should be updated
	// Re-scan to check the new state
	guard2_rescan, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, guard2)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	if guard2_rescan != nil && guard2_rescan.ShouldWait() {
		// Still waiting on something else or lock not fully resolved
		lt.Dequeue(guard2_rescan)
	} else {
		lt.Dequeue(guard2)
	}
}

// TestIntentResolutionAfterAbort verifies that locks are released after
// transaction abort.
func TestIntentResolutionAfterAbort(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to access
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	// Simulate first transaction abort - release locks
	lt.Dequeue(guard1)

	// Rescan to check that second transaction can now proceed
	guard2_rescan, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, guard2)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	if guard2_rescan != nil {
		lt.Dequeue(guard2_rescan)
	} else {
		lt.Dequeue(guard2)
	}
}

// TestMultipleWritersQueueing verifies that multiple writers form a queue
// waiting for a lock.
func TestMultipleWritersQueueing(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Create three transactions
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn3 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1002},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1002},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction waits
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	// Third transaction also waits
	req3 := Request{
		Txn:       txn3,
		Timestamp: txn3.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req3.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard3, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req3, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard3.ShouldWait())

	// Verify queue depth
	state3, err := guard3.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state3.kind)
	// The queue should have other waiters
	require.Greater(t, state3.queuedLockingRequests, 0)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
	lt.Dequeue(guard3)
}

// TestLockStrengthHierarchy verifies that different lock strengths
// interact correctly.
func TestLockStrengthHierarchy(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires exclusive (intent) lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to acquire shared lock
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Shared, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	// Shared lock should conflict with Intent lock
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestTransactionEpochsAndLocks verifies that lock table correctly handles
// transaction epochs.
func TestTransactionEpochsAndLocks(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Transaction with epoch 1
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Same transaction with epoch 2 (restart)
	txn1_epoch2 := txn1.Clone()
	txn1_epoch2.Epoch = 2

	req1_epoch2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1_epoch2,
		Timestamp: txn1_epoch2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1_epoch2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Scanning with the same transaction but higher epoch should find the old lock
	guard1_epoch2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1_epoch2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	// Should not wait for its own lock
	require.False(t, guard1_epoch2.ShouldWait())

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard1_epoch2)
}

// TestIsolationLevelConflicts verifies that different isolation levels
// affect conflict detection.
func TestIsolationLevelConflicts(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Serializable transaction writes
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// Read committed transaction tries to read
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.ReadCommitted,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Read committed transaction's read should still wait for the write lock
	// Lock conflicts are independent of isolation level
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Shared, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// Helper function to create a span
func span(start, end roachpb.Key) roachpb.Span {
	return roachpb.Span{Key: start, EndKey: end}
}

// Copyright 2020 The Cockroach Authors.

// included in the /LICENSE file.

package concurrency

import (
	"context"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvpb"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/concurrency/isolation"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/concurrency/lock"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/lockspanset"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/spanset"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/txnwait"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/roachpb"
	clustersettings "github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/settings/cluster"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/storage/enginepb"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/hlc"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/leaktest"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/log"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/uuid"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// TestWriteWriteConflictDetection verifies that the lock table correctly detects
// write-write conflicts between two transactions.
func TestWriteWriteConflictDetection(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	// Create a lock table with capacity for this test
	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	// Create two transactions with different UUIDs
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// Create requests for the same key with intent locks
	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// First request: txn1 writes to key1
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Second request: txn2 writes to the same key1
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Scan and enqueue first request
	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, guard1)
	require.False(t, guard1.ShouldWait(), "first request should not wait")

	// Acquire lock for first request
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Scan and enqueue second request - should find conflict
	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, guard2)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "second request should wait for first transaction's lock")

	// Check the waiting state
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state.kind)
	require.Equal(t, txn1.ID, state.txn.ID)
	require.True(t, state.held)

	// Release the locks
	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestReadWriteConflictWithSerializableIsolation verifies that serializable
// transactions detect read-write conflicts.
func TestReadWriteConflictWithSerializableIsolation(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction writes to key
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.False(t, guard1.ShouldWait())

	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to read the same key
	// In serializable isolation, reads should also detect conflicts with writes
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Shared, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, guard2)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "read should wait for write lock")

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestTransactionPriorityConflictResolution verifies that transaction priority
// influences conflict resolution.
func TestTransactionPriorityConflictResolution(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Low priority transaction writes first
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.LowUserPriority,
	}

	// High priority transaction tries to write to the same key
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.HighUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction (low priority) acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction (high priority) tries to write
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// High priority transaction should wait, but could potentially push low priority
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, txn1.ID, state.txn.ID)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestLockAcquisitionTimout verifies that lock acquisition can timeout.
func TestLockAcquisitionTimeout(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to acquire with a timeout
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:            txn2,
		Timestamp:      txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:      lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans:     spanset.SpanSet{},
		LockWaitPolicy: lock.WaitPolicy_Block,
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	// In a real scenario with a deadline, the wait would eventually timeout
	// For this test, we just verify the wait state is set up correctly
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state.kind)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestIntentResolutionAfterCommit verifies that locks are released after
// transaction commit.
func TestIntentResolutionAfterCommit(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to access the same key
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "should wait for first transaction's lock")

	// Release the lock from first transaction (simulating commit)
	lt.Dequeue(guard1)

	// After dequeue, second transaction's wait state should be updated
	// Re-scan to check the new state
	guard2_rescan, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, guard2)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	if guard2_rescan != nil && guard2_rescan.ShouldWait() {
		// Still waiting on something else or lock not fully resolved
		lt.Dequeue(guard2_rescan)
	} else {
		lt.Dequeue(guard2)
	}
}

func TestIntentResolutionAfterAbort(t *testing.T) {

		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{

	}

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to access
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)

// TestMultipleWritersQueueing verifies that multiple writers form a queue
// waiting for a lock.
func TestMultipleWritersQueueing(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Create three transactions
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn3 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1002},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1002},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction waits
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	// Third transaction also waits
	req3 := Request{
		Txn:       txn3,
		Timestamp: txn3.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req3.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard3, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req3, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard3.ShouldWait())

	// Verify queue depth
	state3, err := guard3.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state3.kind)
	// The queue should have other waiters
	require.Greater(t, state3.queuedLockingRequests, 0)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
	lt.Dequeue(guard3)
}

// TestLockStrengthHierarchy verifies that different lock strengths
// interact correctly.
func TestLockStrengthHierarchy(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires exclusive (intent) lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to acquire shared lock
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Shared, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	// Shared lock should conflict with Intent lock
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestTransactionEpochsAndLocks verifies that lock table correctly handles
// transaction epochs.
func TestTransactionEpochsAndLocks(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Transaction with epoch 1
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Same transaction with epoch 2 (restart)
	txn1_epoch2 := txn1.Clone()
	txn1_epoch2.Epoch = 2

	req1_epoch2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1_epoch2,
		Timestamp: txn1_epoch2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1_epoch2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Scanning with the same transaction but higher epoch should find the old lock
	guard1_epoch2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1_epoch2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	// Should not wait for its own lock
	require.False(t, guard1_epoch2.ShouldWait())

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard1_epoch2)
}

// TestIsolationLevelConflicts verifies that different isolation levels
// affect conflict detection.
func TestIsolationLevelConflicts(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Serializable transaction writes
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// Read committed transaction tries to read
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.ReadCommitted,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Read committed transaction's read should still wait for the write lock
	// Lock conflicts are independent of isolation level
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Shared, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// Helper function to create a span
func span(start, end roachpb.Key) roachpb.Span {
	return roachpb.Span{Key: start, EndKey: end}
}

// TestWriteWriteConflictDetection verifies that the lock table correctly detects
// write-write conflicts between two transactions.
func TestWriteWriteConflictDetection(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	// Create a lock table with capacity for this test
	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	// Create two transactions with different UUIDs
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// Create requests for the same key with intent locks
	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// First request: txn1 writes to key1
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Second request: txn2 writes to the same key1
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Scan and enqueue first request
	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, guard1)
	require.False(t, guard1.ShouldWait(), "first request should not wait")

	// Acquire lock for first request
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Scan and enqueue second request - should find conflict
	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, guard2)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "second request should wait for first transaction's lock")

	// Check the waiting state
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state.kind)
	require.Equal(t, txn1.ID, state.txn.ID)
	require.True(t, state.held)

	// Release the locks
	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestReadWriteConflictWithSerializableIsolation verifies that serializable
// transactions detect read-write conflicts.
func TestReadWriteConflictWithSerializableIsolation(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction writes to key
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.False(t, guard1.ShouldWait())

	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to read the same key
	// In serializable isolation, reads should also detect conflicts with writes
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},

	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "read should wait for write lock")
