}

// TestTransactionPriorityConflictResolution verifies that transaction priority
// influences conflict resolution.
func TestTransactionPriorityConflictResolution(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Low priority transaction writes first
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.LowUserPriority,
	}

	// High priority transaction tries to write to the same key
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.HighUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction (low priority) acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction (high priority) tries to write
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// High priority transaction should wait, but could potentially push low priority
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, txn1.ID, state.txn.ID)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestLockAcquisitionTimout verifies that lock acquisition can timeout.
func TestLockAcquisitionTimeout(t *testing.T) {

	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to acquire with a timeout
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:            txn2,
		Timestamp:      txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:      lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans:     spanset.SpanSet{},
		LockWaitPolicy: lock.WaitPolicy_Block,
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	// In a real scenario with a deadline, the wait would eventually timeout
	// For this test, we just verify the wait state is set up correctly
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state.kind)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestIntentResolutionAfterCommit verifies that locks are released after
// transaction commit.
func TestIntentResolutionAfterCommit(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to access the same key
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "should wait for first transaction's lock")

	// Release the lock from first transaction (simulating commit)
	lt.Dequeue(guard1)

	// After dequeue, second transaction's wait state should be updated
	// Re-scan to check the new state
	guard2_rescan, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, guard2)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	if guard2_rescan != nil && guard2_rescan.ShouldWait() {
		// Still waiting on something else or lock not fully resolved
		lt.Dequeue(guard2_rescan)
	} else {
		lt.Dequeue(guard2)
	}
}

// TestMultipleWritersQueueing verifies that multiple writers form a queue
// waiting for a lock.
func TestMultipleWritersQueueing(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn3 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1002},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1002},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction waits
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	// Third transaction also waits
	req3 := Request{
		Txn:       txn3,
		Timestamp: txn3.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req3.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard3, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req3, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard3.ShouldWait())

	// Verify queue depth
	state3, err := guard3.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state3.kind)
	// The queue should have other waiters
	require.Greater(t, state3.queuedLockingRequests, 0)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
	lt.Dequeue(guard3)
}

// TestTransactionEpochsAndLocks verifies that lock table correctly handles
// transaction epochs.
func TestTransactionEpochsAndLocks(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Transaction with epoch 1
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Same transaction with epoch 2 (restart)
	txn1_epoch2 := txn1.Clone()
	txn1_epoch2.Epoch = 2

	req1_epoch2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1_epoch2,
		Timestamp: txn1_epoch2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1_epoch2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Scanning with the same transaction but higher epoch should find the old lock
	guard1_epoch2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1_epoch2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	// Should not wait for its own lock
	require.False(t, guard1_epoch2.ShouldWait())

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard1_epoch2)
}

// TestIsolationLevelConflicts verifies that different isolation levels
// affect conflict detection.
func TestIsolationLevelConflicts(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Serializable transaction writes
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// Read committed transaction tries to read
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.ReadCommitted,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Read committed transaction's read should still wait for the write lock
	// Lock conflicts are independent of isolation level
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Shared, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// Helper function to create a span
func span(start, end roachpb.Key) roachpb.Span {
	return roachpb.Span{Key: start, EndKey: end}

215: 	// under. Discovered locks from prior lease sequences are ignored, as they
216: 	// may no longer be accurate.
217: 	enabled    bool
218: 	enabledMu  syncutil.RWMutex
219: 	enabledSeq roachpb.LeaseSequence
220: 
221: 	// A sequence number is assigned to each request seen by the lockTable. This
222: 	// is to preserve fairness despite the design choice of allowing
223: 	// out-of-order evaluation of requests with overlapping spans where the
224: 	// latter request does not encounter contention. This out-of-order
225: 	// evaluation happens because requests do not reserve spans that are
226: 	// uncontended while they wait for on contended locks after releasing their
227: 	// latches. Consider the following examples:
228: 	//
229: 	// Example 1:
230: 	// - req1 wants to write to A, B
231: 	// - req2 wants to write to B
232: 	// - lock at A is held by some other txn.
233: 	// - Even though req2 arrives later, req1 will wait only in the queue for A
234: 	//   and allow req2 to proceed to evaluation.
235: 	//
236: 	// Example 2:
237: 	// - Same as example 1 but lock at A is held by txn3 and lock at B is held
238: 	//   by txn4.
239: 	// - Lock at A is released so req1 claims the lock at A and starts waiting at
240: 	//   B.
241: 	// - It is unfair for req1 to wait behind req2 at B. The sequence number
242: 	//   assigned to req1 and req2 will restore the fairness by making req1
243: 	//   wait before req2.
244: 	//
245: 	// Example 3: Deadlock in lock table if it did not use sequence numbers.
246: 	// - Lock at B is acquired by txn0.
247: 	// - req1 (from txn1) arrives at lockTable and wants to write to A and B.
248: 	//   It queues at B.
249: 	// - req2 (from txn2) arrives at lockTable and only wants to write A.
250: 	//   It proceeds to evaluation and acquires the lock at A for txn2 and then
251: 	//   the request is done. The lock is still held.
252: 	// - req3 (from txn3) wants to write to A and B. It queues at A.
253: 	// - txn2 releases A. req3 is in the front of the queue at A so it claims the
254: 	//   lock and starts waiting at B behind req1.
255: 	// - txn0 releases B. req1 gets to claim the lock at B and does another scan
256: 	//   and adds itself to the queue at A, behind req3 which holds the claim for
257: 	//   A.
258: 	// Now in the queues for A and B req1 is behind req3 and vice versa and
259: 	// this deadlock has been created entirely due to the lock table's behavior.
260: 	seqNum atomic.Uint64
261: 
262: 	// locks contains the btree object (wrapped in the treeMu structure) that
263: 	// contains the actual keyLocks objects. These keyLocks objects represent the
264: 	// individual keys (that may have one or more locks on them) in the lock
265: 	// table. Locks on both Global and Local keys are stored in the same btree.
266: 	locks treeMu
267: 
268: 	// maxKeysLocked is a soft maximum on amount of per-key lock information
269: 	// tracking[1]. When it is exceeded, and subject to the dampening in
270: 	// lockAddMaxLocksCheckInterval, locks will be cleared.
271: 	//
272: 	// [1] Simply put, the number of keyLocks objects in the lockTable btree.
273: 	maxKeysLocked int64
274: 	// When maxKeysLocked is exceeded, will attempt to clear down to minKeysLocked,
275: 	// instead of clearing everything.
276: 	minKeysLocked int64
277: 
278: 	// txnStatusCache is a small LRU cache that tracks the status of
279: 	// transactions that have been successfully pushed.
280: 	//
281: 	// NOTE: it probably makes sense to maintain a single txnStatusCache
282: 	// across all Ranges on a Store instead of an individual cache per
283: 	// Range. For now, we don't do this because we don't share any state
284: 	// between separate concurrency.Manager instances.
285: 	txnStatusCache txnStatusCache
286: 
287: 	// clock is used to track the lock hold and lock wait start times.
288: 	clock *hlc.Clock
289: 
290: 	// settings provides a handle to cluster settings.
291: 	settings *cluster.Settings
292: }
293: 
294: var _ lockTable = &lockTableImpl{}
295: 
296: func newLockTable(
297: 	maxLocks int64, rangeID roachpb.RangeID, clock *hlc.Clock, settings *cluster.Settings,
298: ) *lockTableImpl {
299: 	lt := &lockTableImpl{
300: 		rID:      rangeID,
301: 		clock:    clock,
302: 		settings: settings,
303: 	}
304: 	lt.setMaxKeysLocked(maxLocks)
305: 	return lt
306: }
307: 
308: func (t *lockTableImpl) setMaxKeysLocked(maxKeysLocked int64) {
309: 	// Check at 5% intervals of the max count.
310: 	lockAddMaxLocksCheckInterval := maxKeysLocked / int64(20)
311: 	if lockAddMaxLocksCheckInterval == 0 {
312: 		lockAddMaxLocksCheckInterval = 1
313: 	}
314: 	t.maxKeysLocked = maxKeysLocked
315: 	t.minKeysLocked = maxKeysLocked / 2
316: 	t.locks.lockAddMaxLocksCheckInterval = uint64(lockAddMaxLocksCheckInterval)
317: }
318: 
319: // lockTableGuardImpl is an implementation of lockTableGuard.
320: //
321: // The struct is a guard that is returned to the request the first time it calls
322: // lockTable.ScanAndEnqueue() and used in later calls to ScanAndEnqueue() and
323: // done(). After a call to ScanAndEnqueue() (which is made while holding
324: // latches), the caller must first call lockTableGuard.StartWaiting() and if it
325: // returns true release the latches and continue interacting with the
326: // lockTableGuard. If StartWaiting() returns false, the request can proceed to
327: // evaluation.
328: //
329: // Waiting logic: The interface hides the queues that the request is waiting on,
330: // and the request's position in the queue. One of the reasons for this hiding
331: // is that queues are not FIFO since a request that did not wait on a queue for
332: // key k in a preceding call to ScanAndEnqueue() (because k was not locked and
333: // there was no queue) may need to wait on the queue in a later call to
334: // ScanAndEnqueue(). So sequencing of requests arriving at the lockTable is
335: // partially decided by a sequence number assigned to a request when it first
336: // called ScanAndEnqueue() and queues are ordered by this sequence number.
337: // However the sequencing is not fully described by the sequence numbers -- a
338: // request R1 encountering contention over some keys in its span does not
339: // prevent a request R2 that has a higher sequence number and overlapping span
340: // to proceed if R2 does not encounter contention. This concurrency (that is not
341: // completely fair) is deemed desirable.
342: //
343: // The interface exposes an abstracted version of the waiting logic in a way
344: // that the request that starts waiting is considered waiting for at most one
345: // other request or transaction. This is exposed as a series of state
346: // transitions where the transitions are notified via newState() and the current
347: // state can be read using CurState().
348: //
349: //   - The waitFor* states provide information on who the request is waiting
350: //     for.
351: //
352: //   - The waitElsewhere state is a rare state that is used when the lockTable is
353: //     under memory pressure and is clearing its internal queue state. Like the
354: //     waitFor* states, it informs the request who it is waiting for so that
355: //     deadlock detection works. However, sequencing information inside the
356: //     lockTable is mostly discarded.
357: //
358: //   - The waitSelf state is a rare state when a different request from the same
359: //     transaction has claimed the lock. See the comment about the concept of
360: //     claiming a lock on claimantTxn().
361: //
362: //   - The waitQueueMaxLengthExceeded state is used to indicate that the request
363: //     was rejected because it attempted to enter a lock wait-queue as a locking
364: //     request and found that the queue's length was already equal to or
365: //     exceeding the request's configured maximum.
366: //
367: //   - The doneWaiting state is used to indicate that the request should make
368: //     another call to ScanAndEnqueue() (that next call is more likely to return a
369: //     lockTableGuard that returns false from StartWaiting()).
370: type lockTableGuardImpl struct {
371: 	seqNum uint64
372: 	lt     *lockTableImpl
373: 
374: 	// Information about this request.
375: 	txn                *roachpb.Transaction
376: 	ts                 hlc.Timestamp
377: 	spans              *lockspanset.LockSpanSet
378: 	waitPolicy         lock.WaitPolicy
379: 	maxWaitQueueLength int
380: 
381: 	// Snapshot of the tree for which this request has some spans. Note that
382: 	// the lockStates in this snapshot may have been removed from
383: 	// lockTableImpl. Additionally, it is possible that there is a new keyLocks
384: 	// struct for the same key. This can result in various harmless anomalies:
385: 	// - the request may hold a claim on a keyLocks struct that is no longer
386: 	//   in the tree. When it next does a scan, it will either find a new
387: 	//   keyLocks struct where it will compete or none. Both lockStates can be in
388: 	//   the mu.locks map, which is harmless.
389: 	// - the request may wait behind a transaction that has claimed a lock but is
390: 	//   yet to acquire it. This could cause a delay in pushing the lock holder.
391: 	//   This is not a correctness issue (the whole system is not deadlocked) and we
392: 	//   expect will not be a real performance issue.
393: 	//
394: 	// TODO(sbhola): experimentally evaluate the lazy queueing of the current
395: 	// implementation, in comparison with eager queueing. If eager queueing
396: 	// is comparable in system throughput, one can eliminate the above anomalies.
397: 	//
398: 	// TODO(nvanbenschoten): should we be Reset-ing these btree snapshot when we
399: 	// Dequeue a lockTableGuardImpl? In releaseLockTableGuardImpl?
400: 	//

6: // Package concurrency provides a concurrency manager structure that
7: // encapsulates the details of concurrency control and contention handling for
8: // serializable key-value transactions.
9: package concurrency
10:

12: 	"context"
13: 	"time"

26: )
27: 
28: // Manager is a structure that sequences incoming requests and provides
29: // isolation between requests that intend to perform conflicting operations.
30: // During sequencing, conflicts are discovered and any found are resolved
31: // through a combination of passive queuing and active pushing. Once a request
32: // has been sequenced, it is free to evaluate without concerns of conflicting
33: // with other in-flight requests due to the isolation provided by the manager.
34: // This isolation is guaranteed for the lifetime of the request but terminates
35: // once the request completes.
36: //
37: // Transactions require isolation both within requests and across requests. The
38: // manager accommodates this by allowing transactional requests to acquire
39: // locks, which outlive the requests
...

{"content":"580: //   - a request that is part of a transaction which has already acquired a lock
581: //     does not need to wait on that lock during sequencing, and can therefore
582: //     ignore any queue that has formed on the lock.
583: //
584: //   - contending requests that encounter different levels of contention may be
585: //     sequenced in non-FIFO order. This is to allow for more concurrency. e.g.
586: //     if request R1 and R2 contend on key K2, but R1 is also waiting at key K1,
587: //     R2 could slip past R1 and evaluate.
588: type lockTable interface {
589: 	requestQueuer
590: 
591: 	// ScanAndEnqueue scans over the spans that the request will access and
592: 	// enqueues the request in the lock wait-queue of any conflicting locks
593: 	// encountered.
594: 	//
595: 	// The first call to ScanAndEnqueue for a given request uses a nil
596: 	// lockTableGuard and the subsequent calls reuse the previously returned
597: 	// one. The latches needed by the request must be held when calling this
598: 	// function.
599: 	ScanAndEnqueue(Request, lockTableGuard) (lockTableGuard, *Error)
600: 
601: 	// ScanOptimistic takes a snapshot of the lock table for later checking for
602: 	// conflicts, and returns a guard. It is for optimistic evaluation of
603: 	// requests that will typically scan a small subset of the spans mentioned
604: 	// in the Request. After Request evaluation, CheckOptimisticNoConflicts
605: 	// must be called on the guard.
606: 	ScanOptimistic(Request) lockTableGuard
607: 
608: 	// Dequeue removes the request from its lock wait-queues. It should be
609: 	// called when the request is finished, whether it evaluated or not. The
610: 	// guard should not be used after being dequeued.
611: 	//
612: 	// This method does not release any locks. This method must be called on the
613: 	// last guard returned from ScanAndEnqueue for the request, even if one of
614: 	// the (a) lockTable calls that use a lockTableGuard parameter, or (b) a
615: 	// lockTableGuard call, returned an error. The method allows but does not
616: 	// require latches to be held.
617: 	Dequeue(lockTableGuard)
618: 
619: 	// AddDiscoveredLock informs the lockTable of a lock which is wasn't
620: 	// previously tracking that was discovered during evaluation under the
621: 	// provided lease sequence.
622: 	//
623: 	// The method is called when an exclusive replicated lock held by a
624: 	// different transaction is discovered when reading the MVCC keys during
625: 	// evaluation of this request. It adds the lock and enqueues this requester
626: 	// in its wait-queue. It is required that request evaluation discover such
627: 	// locks before acquiring its own locks, since the request needs to repeat
628: 	// ScanAndEnqueue. When consultTxnStatusCache=true, and the transaction
629: 	// holding the lock is known to be pushed or finalized, the lock is not added
630: 	// to the lock table and instead tracked in the list of locks to resolve in
631: 	// the lockTableGuard.
632: 	//
633: 	// The lease sequence is used to detect lease changes between the when
634: 	// request that found the lock started evaluating and when the discovered
635: 	// lock is added to the lockTable. If there has been a lease change between
636: 	// these times, information about the discovered lock may be stale, so it is
637: 	// ignored.
638: 	//
639: 	// A latch consistent with the access desired by the guard must be held on
640: 	// the span containing the discovered lock's key.
641: 	//
642: 	// The method returns a boolean indicating whether the discovered lock was
643: 	// properly handled either by adding it to the lockTable or storing it in
644: 	// the list of locks to resolve in the lockTableGuard (both cases return
645: 	// true) or whether it was ignored because the lockTable is currently
646: 	// disabled (false).
647: 	AddDiscoveredLock(
648: 		foundLock *roachpb.Lock, seq roachpb.LeaseSequence,
649: 		consultTxnStatusCache bool, guard lockTableGuard,
650: 	) (bool, error)
651: 
652: 	// AcquireLock informs the lockTable that a new lock was acquired or an
653: 	// existing lock was updated.
654: 	//
655: 	// The TxnMeta associated with the lock acquisition must be the same one used
656: 	// when the request scanned the lockTable initially. It must only be called in
657: 	// the evaluation phase, before calling Dequeue, which means all latches
658: 	// needed by the request are held. The key must be in the request's lock span
659: 	// set with the appropriate strength. Currently, the only strength with which a
660: 	// lock can be acquired is Intent[1]. This contract ensures that the lock is
661: 	// not held in a conflicting manner by a different transaction.
662: 	// Acquiring a lock that is already held by a transaction upgrades the lock's
663: 	// timestamp and strength. Any prior sequence numbers at which the lock was
664: 	// previously held and has since been rolled back are no longer tracked as
665: 	// well.
666: 	//
667: 	// [1] We are in an intermediate state where KV ignores lock strength supplied
668: 	// by SQL and acquires all locks with Intent locking strength. Notably, this
669: 	// includes in-memory Exclusive locks acquired for `SELECT FOR UPDATE` queries
670: 	// as well. While the Intent strength verbiage might be a bit surprising,
671: 	// there is no meaningful difference when it comes to conflict resolution
672: 	// between a lock and a request. This is a temporary state, which is bound to
673: 	// change as we go about supporting additional locking strengths in the lock
674: 	// table.
675: 	//
676: 	// For replicated locks, this must be called after the corresponding write
677: 	// intent has been applied to the replicated state machine.
678: 	AcquireLock(*roachpb.LockAcquisition) error
679: 
680: 	// UpdateLocks informs the lockTable that an existing lock or range of locks
681: 	// was either updated or released.
682: 	//
683: 	// The method is called during intent resolution. For spans containing
684: 	// Replicated locks, this must be called after intent resolution has been
685: 	// applied to the replicated state machine. The method itself, however,
686: 	// ignores the Durability field in the LockUpdate. It can therefore be
687: 	// used to update locks for a given transaction for all durability levels.
688: 	//
689: 	// A latch with SpanReadWrite must be held on span with the lowest timestamp
690: 	// at which any of the locks could be held. This is explained below.
691: 	//
692: 	// Note that spans can be wider than the actual keys on which locks were
693: 	// acquired, and it is ok if no locks are found or locks held by other
694: 	// transactions are found (for those lock this call is a noop).
695: 	//
696: 	// For COMMITTED or ABORTED transactions, all locks are released.
697: 	//
698: 	// For PENDING or STAGING transactions, the behavior is:
699: 	//
700: 	// - All replicated locks known to the lockTable are dropped. This is not

package concurrency

import (
	"context"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvpb"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/concurrency/isolation"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/concurrency/lock"

	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/roachpb"
	clustersettings "github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/settings/cluster"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/storage/enginepb"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/hlc"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/leaktest"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/log"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/uuid"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// TestWriteWriteConflictDetection verifies that the lock table correctly detects
// write-write conflicts between two transactions.
func TestWriteWriteConflictDetection(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	// Create a lock table with capacity for this test
	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	// Create two transactions with different UUIDs
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// Create requests for the same key with intent locks
	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// First request: txn1 writes to key1
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Second request: txn2 writes to the same key1
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Scan and enqueue first request
	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, guard1)
	require.False(t, guard1.ShouldWait(), "first request should not wait")

	// Acquire lock for first request
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Scan and enqueue second request - should find conflict
	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, guard2)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "second request should wait for first transaction's lock")

	// Check the waiting state
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state.kind)
	require.Equal(t, txn1.ID, state.txn.ID)
	require.True(t, state.held)

	// Release the locks
	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestReadWriteConflictWithSerializableIsolation verifies that serializable
// transactions detect read-write conflicts.
func TestReadWriteConflictWithSerializableIsolation(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	ctx := context.Background()
	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction writes to key
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.False(t, guard1.ShouldWait())

	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to read the same key
	// In serializable isolation, reads should also detect conflicts with writes
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Shared, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, guard2)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "read should wait for write lock")

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestTransactionPriorityConflictResolution verifies that transaction priority
// influences conflict resolution.
func TestTransactionPriorityConflictResolution(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Low priority transaction writes first
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.LowUserPriority,
	}

	// High priority transaction tries to write to the same key
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.HighUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction (low priority) acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction (high priority) tries to write
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// High priority transaction should wait, but could potentially push low priority
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, txn1.ID, state.txn.ID)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestLockAcquisitionTimout verifies that lock acquisition can timeout.
func TestLockAcquisitionTimeout(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to acquire with a timeout
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:            txn2,
		Timestamp:      txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:      lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans:     spanset.SpanSet{},
		LockWaitPolicy: lock.WaitPolicy_Block,
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	// In a real scenario with a deadline, the wait would eventually timeout
	// For this test, we just verify the wait state is set up correctly
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state.kind)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestIntentResolutionAfterCommit verifies that locks are released after
// transaction commit.
func TestIntentResolutionAfterCommit(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to access the same key
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "should wait for first transaction's lock")

	// Release the lock from first transaction (simulating commit)
	lt.Dequeue(guard1)

	// After dequeue, second transaction's wait state should be updated
	// Re-scan to check the new state
	guard2_rescan, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, guard2)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	if guard2_rescan != nil && guard2_rescan.ShouldWait() {
		// Still waiting on something else or lock not fully resolved
		lt.Dequeue(guard2_rescan)
	} else {
		lt.Dequeue(guard2)
	}
}

// TestIntentResolutionAfterAbort verifies that locks are released after
// transaction abort.
func TestIntentResolutionAfterAbort(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to access
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	// Simulate first transaction abort - release locks
	lt.Dequeue(guard1)

	// Rescan to check that second transaction can now proceed
	guard2_rescan, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, guard2)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	if guard2_rescan != nil {
		lt.Dequeue(guard2_rescan)
	} else {
		lt.Dequeue(guard2)
	}
}

// TestMultipleWritersQueueing verifies that multiple writers form a queue
// waiting for a lock.
func TestMultipleWritersQueueing(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Create three transactions
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn3 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1002},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1002},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction waits
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	// Third transaction also waits
	req3 := Request{
		Txn:       txn3,
		Timestamp: txn3.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req3.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard3, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req3, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard3.ShouldWait())

	// Verify queue depth
	state3, err := guard3.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state3.kind)
	// The queue should have other waiters
	require.Greater(t, state3.queuedLockingRequests, 0)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
	lt.Dequeue(guard3)
}

// TestLockStrengthHierarchy verifies that different lock strengths
// interact correctly.
func TestLockStrengthHierarchy(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// First transaction acquires exclusive (intent) lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to acquire shared lock
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Shared, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	// Shared lock should conflict with Intent lock
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestTransactionEpochsAndLocks verifies that lock table correctly handles
// transaction epochs.
func TestTransactionEpochsAndLocks(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Transaction with epoch 1
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Same transaction with epoch 2 (restart)
	txn1_epoch2 := txn1.Clone()
	txn1_epoch2.Epoch = 2

	req1_epoch2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1_epoch2,
		Timestamp: txn1_epoch2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1_epoch2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Scanning with the same transaction but higher epoch should find the old lock
	guard1_epoch2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1_epoch2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	// Should not wait for its own lock
	require.False(t, guard1_epoch2.ShouldWait())

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard1_epoch2)
}

// TestIsolationLevelConflicts verifies that different isolation levels
// affect conflict detection.
func TestIsolationLevelConflicts(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Serializable transaction writes
	txn1 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	// Read committed transaction tries to read
	txn2 := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.ReadCommitted,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	req1 := Request{
		Txn:       txn1,
		Timestamp: txn1.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(txn1, lock.Intent, guard1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Read committed transaction's read should still wait for the write lock
	// Lock conflicts are independent of isolation level
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:       txn2,
		Timestamp: txn2.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans: lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Shared, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// Helper function to create a span
func span(start, end roachpb.Key) roachpb.Span {
	return roachpb.Span{Key: start, EndKey: end}
}

// Copyright 2024 The Cockroach Authors.

package concurrency

import (
	"context"
	"testing"

	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvpb"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/concurrency/isolation"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/concurrency/lock"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/lockspanset"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/kv/kvserver/spanset"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/roachpb"
	clustersettings "github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/settings/cluster"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/storage/enginepb"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/hlc"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/leaktest"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/log"
	"github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/pkg/util/uuid"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// TestWriteWriteConflictDetection verifies that the lock table correctly detects
// write-write conflicts between two transactions.
func TestWriteWriteConflictDetection(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	// Create a lock table with capacity for this test
	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	ctx := context.Background()

	// Create two transactions with different UUIDs
	txn1Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	txn1 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn1Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	txn2Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}

	txn2 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn2Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	// Create requests for the same key with intent locks
	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// First request: txn1 writes to key1
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:        txn1,
		Timestamp:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Second request: txn2 writes to the same key1
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:        txn2,
		Timestamp:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Scan and enqueue first request
	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, guard1)
	require.False(t, guard1.ShouldWait(), "first request should not wait")

	// Acquire lock for first request
	acq1 := roachpb.MakeLockAcquisition(txn1Meta, key, lock.Replicated, lock.Intent, nil)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(&acq1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Scan and enqueue second request - should find conflict
	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, guard2)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "second request should wait for first transaction's lock")

	// Check the waiting state
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state.kind)
	require.Equal(t, txn1Meta.ID, state.txn.ID)
	require.True(t, state.held)

	// Release the locks
	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestReadWriteConflictWithSerializableIsolation verifies that serializable
// transactions detect read-write conflicts.
func TestReadWriteConflictWithSerializableIsolation(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn1 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn1Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	txn2Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn2Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	// First transaction writes to key
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:        txn1,
		Timestamp:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.False(t, guard1.ShouldWait())

	acq1 := roachpb.MakeLockAcquisition(txn1Meta, key, lock.Replicated, lock.Intent, nil)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(&acq1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to read the same key
	// In serializable isolation, reads should also detect conflicts with writes
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:        txn2,
		Timestamp:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Shared, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, guard2)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "read should wait for write lock")

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestTransactionPriorityConflictResolution verifies that transaction priority
// influences conflict resolution.
func TestTransactionPriorityConflictResolution(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Low priority transaction writes first
	txn1Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.LowUserPriority,
	}
	txn1 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn1Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	// High priority transaction tries to write to the same key
	txn2Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.HighUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn2Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	// First transaction (low priority) acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:        txn1,
		Timestamp:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	acq1 := roachpb.MakeLockAcquisition(txn1Meta, key, lock.Replicated, lock.Intent, nil)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(&acq1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction (high priority) tries to write
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:        txn2,
		Timestamp:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// High priority transaction should wait, but could potentially push low priority
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, txn1Meta.ID, state.txn.ID)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestLockAcquisitionTimeout verifies that lock acquisition can timeout.
func TestLockAcquisitionTimeout(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn1 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn1Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	txn2Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn2Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:        txn1,
		Timestamp:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	acq1 := roachpb.MakeLockAcquisition(txn1Meta, key, lock.Replicated, lock.Intent, nil)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(&acq1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to acquire with a wait policy
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:            txn2,
		Timestamp:      txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:      lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans:     spanset.SpanSet{},
		LockWaitPolicy: lock.WaitPolicy_Block,
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	// In a real scenario with a deadline, the wait would eventually timeout
	// For this test, we just verify the wait state is set up correctly
	state, err := guard2.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state.kind)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestIntentResolutionAfterCommit verifies that locks are released after
// transaction commit.
func TestIntentResolutionAfterCommit(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn1 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn1Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	txn2Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn2Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:        txn1,
		Timestamp:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	acq1 := roachpb.MakeLockAcquisition(txn1Meta, key, lock.Replicated, lock.Intent, nil)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(&acq1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to access the same key
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:        txn2,
		Timestamp:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait(), "should wait for first transaction's lock")

	// Release the lock from first transaction (simulating commit)
	lockUpdate := &roachpb.LockUpdate{
		Span:   span(key, key.Next()),
		Txn:    txn1Meta.TxnMeta(),
		Status: roachpb.COMMITTED,
	}
	err = lt.UpdateLocks(lockUpdate)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestIntentResolutionAfterAbort verifies that locks are released after
// transaction abort.
func TestIntentResolutionAfterAbort(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn1 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn1Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	txn2Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn2Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:        txn1,
		Timestamp:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	acq1 := roachpb.MakeLockAcquisition(txn1Meta, key, lock.Replicated, lock.Intent, nil)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(&acq1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to access
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:        txn2,
		Timestamp:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	// Simulate first transaction abort - release locks
	lockUpdate := &roachpb.LockUpdate{
		Span:   span(key, key.Next()),
		Txn:    txn1Meta.TxnMeta(),
		Status: roachpb.ABORTED,
	}
	err = lt.UpdateLocks(lockUpdate)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestMultipleWritersQueueing verifies that multiple writers form a queue
// waiting for a lock.
func TestMultipleWritersQueueing(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Create three transactions
	txn1Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn1 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn1Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	txn2Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn2Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	txn3Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1002},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1002},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn3 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn3Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn3Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn3Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	// First transaction acquires lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:        txn1,
		Timestamp:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	acq1 := roachpb.MakeLockAcquisition(txn1Meta, key, lock.Replicated, lock.Intent, nil)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(&acq1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction waits
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:        txn2,
		Timestamp:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	// Third transaction also waits
	req3 := Request{
		Txn:        txn3,
		Timestamp:  txn3Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req3.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard3, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req3, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard3.ShouldWait())

	// Verify queue depth
	state3, err := guard3.CurState()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, waitFor, state3.kind)
	// The queue should have other waiters
	require.Greater(t, state3.queuedLockingRequests, 0)

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
	lt.Dequeue(guard3)
}

// TestLockStrengthHierarchy verifies that different lock strengths
// interact correctly.
func TestLockStrengthHierarchy(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	txn1Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn1 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn1Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	txn2Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn2Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	// First transaction acquires exclusive (intent) lock
	req1 := Request{
		Txn:        txn1,
		Timestamp:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	acq1 := roachpb.MakeLockAcquisition(txn1Meta, key, lock.Replicated, lock.Intent, nil)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(&acq1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Second transaction tries to acquire shared lock
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:        txn2,
		Timestamp:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Shared, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	// Shared lock should conflict with Intent lock
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// TestTransactionEpochsAndLocks verifies that lock table correctly handles
// transaction epochs.
func TestTransactionEpochsAndLocks(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Transaction with epoch 1
	txn1Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn1 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn1Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	req1 := Request{
		Txn:        txn1,
		Timestamp:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	acq1 := roachpb.MakeLockAcquisition(txn1Meta, key, lock.Replicated, lock.Intent, nil)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(&acq1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Same transaction with epoch 2 (restart)
	txn1_epoch2Meta := txn1Meta.Clone()
	txn1_epoch2Meta.Epoch = 2
	txn1_epoch2 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn1_epoch2Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn1_epoch2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn1_epoch2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	req1_epoch2 := Request{
		Txn:        txn1_epoch2,
		Timestamp:  txn1_epoch2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1_epoch2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	// Scanning with the same transaction but higher epoch should find the old lock
	guard1_epoch2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1_epoch2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	// Should not wait for its own lock
	require.False(t, guard1_epoch2.ShouldWait())

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard1_epoch2)
}

// TestIsolationLevelConflicts verifies that different isolation levels
// affect conflict detection.
func TestIsolationLevelConflicts(t *testing.T) {
	defer leaktest.AfterTest(t)()
	defer log.Scope(t).Close(t)

	settings := clustersettings.MakeTestingClusterSettings()
	clock := hlc.NewClockForTesting(nil)

	lt := newLockTable(10000, roachpb.RangeID(1), clock, settings)
	lt.enabled = true
	lt.enabledSeq = 1

	key := roachpb.Key("key1")

	// Serializable transaction writes
	txn1Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.Serializable,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1000},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn1 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn1Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	// Read committed transaction tries to read
	txn2Meta := &enginepb.TxnMeta{
		ID:             uuid.Must(uuid.NewV4()),
		IsoLevel:       isolation.ReadCommitted,
		Epoch:          1,
		WriteTimestamp: hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		MinTimestamp:   hlc.Timestamp{WallTime: 1001},
		Priority:       roachpb.NormalUserPriority,
	}
	txn2 := &roachpb.Transaction{
		TxnMeta:                 *txn2Meta,
		ReadTimestamp:           txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		GlobalUncertaintyLimit:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
	}

	req1 := Request{
		Txn:        txn1,
		Timestamp:  txn1Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req1.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Intent, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard1, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	acq1 := roachpb.MakeLockAcquisition(txn1Meta, key, lock.Replicated, lock.Intent, nil)
	err = lt.AcquireLock(&acq1)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Read committed transaction's read should still wait for the write lock
	// Lock conflicts are independent of isolation level
	req2 := Request{
		Txn:        txn2,
		Timestamp:  txn2Meta.WriteTimestamp,
		LockSpans:  lockspanset.LockSpanSet{},
		LatchSpans: spanset.SpanSet{},
	}
	req2.LockSpans.AddLocks(lock.Shared, span(key, key.Next()))

	guard2, err := lt.ScanAndEnqueue(&req2, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, guard2.ShouldWait())

	lt.Dequeue(guard1)
	lt.Dequeue(guard2)
}

// Helper function to create a span
func span(start, end roachpb.Key) roachpb.Span {
	return roachpb.Span{Key: start, EndKey: end}
}

// Copyright 2024 The Cockroach Authors.
//
